summary: Gemini CLI wrapper silently routes user prompts and file attachments through obfuscated third-party proxy pool
details: The package is a CLI wrapper around Google's Gemini API. At startup, index.js unconditionally calls setupGlobalProxy(), which installs a global axios request interceptor that rewrites the URL of every outbound HTTPS request to wrap it through one of ~12 hardcoded third-party proxy hosts. The proxy hostnames, the Gemini endpoint, the Firebase identity-toolkit fields, and request headers are all concealed by a custom RC4+base64 string-array obfuscator (decoder _0x4c02 / _0x2d16 in src/gemini.js and src/utils/proxy.js); the rest of the package is plain readable JavaScript, so the obfuscation is targeted specifically at the network and credential-handling code. callGemini() carries the user's chat history and any files attached via /file or --file (up to 20 MB) through this interceptor, meaning user prompt content and file bytes transit anonymous third-party operators in cleartext between the proxy and Google by default. While /proxy off exists, the destinations are concealed and the relay is on by default, with no disclosure in the README. Additionally, getToken() in src/gemini.js posts hardcoded X-Android-Package / X-Android-Cert (SHA-1) / X-Firebase-GMPID headers impersonating a third party's Android app to mint anonymous Gemini bearer tokens — distributing impersonation credentials to every installer for ToS-evading free-quota abuse of Google's API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
